zh.title: 比特币/美元技术分析：震荡偏强整理
zh.content:
当前BTC/USD处于高位窄幅震荡，价格围绕66300上方运行，多空暂时均衡但偏向多头延续，短线结构维持整理上行格局偏强

SMA20位于66301附近，现价略上穿均线，显示短期支撑有效；MACD柱值为正且扩张，动能仍偏多，但RSI缺失需谨慎超买风险

下方短期支撑位于66220一线，若失守可能回测66150；上方阻力66370及66450，突破需放量确认短线区间压制明显

建议短线以区间交易为主，回踩66250附近可轻仓试多，冲高至阻力区分批止盈，跌破支撑则及时止损观望控制仓位与风险为主执行纪律

en.title: Bitcoin vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
BTC/USD is currently moving in a tight consolidation phase around 66.3k, with price action showing minimal volatility over the last 24 hours. The market remains balanced between buyers and sellers, as neither side has established clear dominance. Despite the lack of directional momentum, the broader structure suggests a mildly bullish bias due to price holding above short-term moving averages and recent stability near local highs.

Indicator readings are mixed but slightly constructive. The MACD at 333.58558 indicates positive momentum, although the absence of a signal line limits confirmation of crossover strength. RSI is unavailable, reducing short-term overbought/oversold clarity. Price trading just above the 20-day SMA at 66301.77 suggests the market is attempting to maintain bullish equilibrium, but momentum remains weak and highly sensitive to intraday fluctuations.

Immediate support is located near the 20-day SMA at 66,301, followed closely by the recent session low at 66,224. A break below this zone could expose the 66,000 psychological level. On the upside, resistance is forming at 66,367, with stronger supply likely near 66,500 and 67,000. The current narrow range suggests a potential breakout setup in either direction. Traders are watching for volume confirmation before positioning.

Trading conditions favor a range-bound approach until a clear breakout confirms direction. Scalpers may consider buying near support zones and selling into resistance, while avoiding over-leveraged positions due to low volatility. A decisive move above 66,500 could signal continuation toward 67,000, whereas a breakdown below 66,200 would shift bias toward deeper retracement. Risk management remains critical in this compressed market structure.
